useBreakpointManager.title: useBreakpointManager Example 1
useBreakpointManager Example 2
useBreakpointManager.code:
<script setup>
  import { ref, computed, provide } from "vue";
  import { useBreakpointManager } from "./useBreakpointManager.js";

  const isMobile = ref(false);

  // Example using the onReady callback to set reactive value isMobile
  // - In this situation isMobile is being used as a primary change in  
  //   the applications layout 
  const options = { 
    onReady(manager) {
      manager.at("small").max(() => isMobile.value = true);
      manager.at("large").min(() => isMobile.value = false);
    }
  };

  // Composable returns the following
  const { 
    /**
     * Reference to underlying BreakpointManager class
    */
    breakpointManager, 
    /**
     * Active breakpoint name
    */
    breakpointActive, 
    /**
     * Current resizeDirection ("up"|"down"|null)
    */
    breakpointDirection 
  } = useBreakpointManager(options);


  // Allowing all components to access these values
  provide("uluBreakpointActive", computed(() => breakpointActive));
  provide("uluBreakpointDirection", computed(() => breakpointDirection));
  provide("uluBreakpointManager", computed(() => breakpointManager));
  provide("uluIsMobile", computed(() => isMobile));

</script>

const uluCore = inject('uluCore');

});

properties.name: cardElement
properties.description:
Specify card element, unless to or href are used which will use 'a' or 'router-link'
- Other than changing to more appropriate element (ie 'li' if in list for example), this can 
  be used to set the card to a button to attach your own click handlers to

properties.name: title
properties.description: Text for title if not using slot

properties.name: titleElement

properties.name: titleHref
properties.description: Will make title a link to href

properties.name: titleTarget
properties.description: When using href this will set title link's target attribute

properties.name: to
properties.description:
If set the entire card will be router link
- Do not us in combination with titleTo or titleHref

properties.name: href
properties.description:
If set the entire card will be a link to href
- Do not us in combination with titleTo or titleHref

properties.name: target
properties.description: When using href this will set link's target attribute

properties.name: classes
properties.description: Classes with class bindings for different elements including ({ title, image })

properties.name: proxyClick
properties.description:
Whether to proxy clicks of non-interactive elements (making whole card clickable).
This is for accessibility, allowing a non-link card to have a primary action.
The proxy action is determined in the following order:
1. If the title has a link (`titleTo` or `titleHref`), it will proxy the click to the title's link.
2. If not, it will look for an element with the `data-ulu-card-proxy-target` attribute within the card's slots and click it.
3. If no proxy target is found, it will emit a `proxy-click` event.
Note: This should not be used with the `to` or `href` props.

properties.name: proxyClickOptions
properties.description: Options to be merged for proxy click settings ({ preventSelector, preventSelectionDuration })

properties.name: image
properties.description:
Advanced configuration for the underlying `<UluImage>` component.
Accepts a full configuration object (e.g., for lazy loading, srcset, etc.).

properties.name: imageSrc
properties.description:
Convenience prop for quickly setting the image source. 
For advanced image configurations, use the `image` prop or `#image` slot instead.

properties.name: imageAlt
properties.description: The alt text for the image when using the `imageSrc` convenience prop.

properties.name: imageIcon
properties.description: If true image will use icon modifier (displays for image adjusts for icon vs full image)

- Can be String or Array of Strings

props.description:
Whether to proxy clicks of non-interactive elements (making whole card clickable).
This is for accessibility, allowing a non-link card to have a primary action.
The proxy action is determined in the following order:
1. If the title has a link (`titleTo` or `titleHref`), it will proxy the click to the title's link.
2. If not, it will look for an element with the `data-ulu-card-proxy-target` attribute within the card's slots and click it.
3. If no proxy target is found, it will emit a `proxy-click` event.
Note: This should not be used with the `to` or `href` props.

### UluFloatingConfig
Prop Type Description

useTableData.raw_markdown: # useTableData A composable for managing and normalizing table data structures. It is used internally by `UluTable` and `UluTableSticky` to handle complex nested column logic, identifier creation, and accessibility attributes. While primarily an internal utility, you can use it to build highly custom table implementations that adhere to the standard `columns` and `rows` data API used by the ULU library. ## Usage ```html ``` ## Parameters The composable accepts two parameters: 1. **`props` (Object)**: An object (usually your component's props) containing the following optional properties: * `columns` (Array): The raw column configuration array. Supports nested columns for complex headers. * `rows` (Array): The raw data rows. * `footerRows` (Array): Raw data rows intended for the table footer. * `idPrefix` (String): A prefix used when generating unique IDs for table headers and cells. 2. **`onChange` (Function)**: An optional callback function that is triggered whenever the `columns`, `rows`, or `footerRows` deeply change. Useful for triggering layout recalculations (like in `UluTableSticky`). ## Returns The composable returns an object containing reactive references: * **`currentColumns` (Ref Array)**: A deeply cloned and normalized version of the `columns` prop. Each column has unique IDs and parent references attached. * **`headerRows` (Ref Array)**: An array of row arrays, calculated based on the maximum depth of nested columns. Used to render the `` with correct `colspan` and `rowspan` values. * **`rowColumns` (ComputedRef Array)**: A flat array representing only the deepest "leaf" columns. Used to iterate over data cells in the ``. Includes utility methods for generating `headers` attributes. * **`currentRows` (Ref Array)**: Normalized rows containing unique IDs and wrapped `data`. * **`currentFooterRows` (Ref Array)**: Normalized footer rows containing unique IDs and wrapped `data`.

useScrollAnchorSections.raw_markdown: # useScrollAnchorSections A composable that provides a reactive list of all registered scroll anchor sections. This is the recommended way to access section data for building custom navigation or other components that need to be aware of the scroll anchor system's state. This composable must be used within a component that is a descendant of `UluScrollAnchors`. ## Usage Here is an example of a simple custom navigation component. ```html ``` ## Parameters The `useScrollAnchorSections` composable takes no parameters. ## Returns The composable returns a reactive `ref` which contains an array of section objects. If used outside of an `UluScrollAnchors` context, it will return `undefined` and log a warning. Each section object in the array has the following properties: - `id` (Symbol): The unique ID of the section. - `title` (String): The title of the section. - `titleId` (String): The ID used for the anchor link. - `element` (HTMLElement): The root HTML element of the section component. - `active` (Boolean): A boolean indicating whether the section is currently active.

useScrollAnchorSection.raw_markdown: # useScrollAnchorSection A composable that provides the core logic for a scroll anchor section, allowing components to register themselves with the `UluScrollAnchors` parent and react to their active state. This composable is intended to be used within a Vue component's ` ``` ## Parameters The `useScrollAnchorSection` composable accepts a `props` object with the following properties: - `title` (String, required): The title of the section. Used for generating a default `titleId` and for display in navigation. - `customTitleId` (String, optional): A custom ID for the section's anchor. If not provided, one will be generated from the `title`. ## Returns The composable returns an object with the following reactive properties: - `sectionRef`: A ref that should be bound to the root HTML element of your section component. This element is observed by `UluScrollAnchors` to determine the section's visibility. - `titleId` (ComputedRef): The unique ID for the section's title, used for anchor links. - `isActive` (ComputedRef): A boolean indicating whether the section is currently active (in view). - `section` (Reactive): The full reactive section object registered with `UluScrollAnchors`, containing `id`, `title`, `titleId`, `element`, and `active` state. ## Integration with `UluScrollAnchors` This composable works in conjunction with the `UluScrollAnchors` component. When a component using `useScrollAnchorSection` is mounted within `UluScrollAnchors`, it automatically registers itself. `UluScrollAnchors` then tracks its visibility and updates the `isActive` state.

facets.raw_markdown:
# Faceted Search This story demonstrates the `useFacets` composable and its associated components. It showcases how to set up a faceted search interface with automatic facet generation, searching, sorting, and filtering. For detailed configuration options, see the [`facetFields` API reference](#facetfields-api) below the example. ```html ```

facetFields API

The `useFacets` composable can automatically generate filter groups from a `facetFields` configuration array. Each object in the array defines a facet group and its behavior. Here are the available keys:
Key Type Default Description
uid String Required The property key on each item in your data array that holds the facet's value.
name String Required The display name for the facet group, shown as a title in the UI.
multiple Boolean false If true, allows multiple values to be selected (renders as checkboxes). If false, only one value can be selected (renders as radio buttons).
open Boolean false If true, the facet group will be expanded by default in the UI.
match String 'some' Only applies when multiple: true. If set to 'all', items must match all selected values in the group (AND logic). The default is to match any value (OR logic).
getValue Function item => item[uid] A function that receives an item and returns the value for the facet. Useful for derived or nested values (e.g., item => item.date.getFullYear()).
getLabel Function value => value A function that receives a facet value and returns a formatted string for display. Useful for transforming values into more readable labels.
